message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Few head-to-head evaluations of immune responses to difference vaccines have been reported.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Surrogate virus neutralization test (sVNT) antibody levels of adults receiving either 2 doses of BNT162b2 (n=366) or CoronaVac (n=360) vaccines in Hong Kong were determined. An age-matched subgroup (BNT162b2 (n=49) vs CoronaVac (n=49)) were tested for plaque reduction neutralizing (PRNT) and spike binding antibody and T cell reactivity in peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    One month after the second dose of vaccine, BNT162b2 elicited significantly higher PRNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    , PRNT
                    <jats:sub>90</jats:sub>
                    , sVNT, spike receptor binding, spike N terminal domain binding, spike S2 domain binding, spike FcR binding and antibody avidity levels than CoronaVac. The geometric mean PRNT
                    <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                    titres in those vaccinated with BNT162b2 and CoronaVac vaccines were 251.6 and 69.45 while PRNT
                    <jats:sub>90</jats:sub>
                    titres were 98.91 and 16.57, respectively. All of those vaccinated with BNT162b2 and 45 (91.8%) of 49 vaccinated with CoronaVac achieved the 50% protection threshold for PRNT
                    <jats:sub>90.</jats:sub>
                    Allowing for an expected seven-fold waning of antibody titres over six months for those receiving CoronaVac, only 16.3% would meet the 50% protection threshold versus 79.6% of BNT162b2 vaccinees. Age was negatively correlated with PRNT
                    <jats:sub>90</jats:sub>
                    antibody titres. Both vaccines induced SARS-CoV-2 specific CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cell responses at 1-month post-vaccination but CoronaVac elicited significantly higher structural protein-specific CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cell responses.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Vaccination with BNT162b2 induces stronger humoral responses than CoronaVac. CoronaVac induce higher CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cell responses to the structural protein than BNT162b2.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Summary At a Glance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Through the head-to-head comparison, vaccination with BNT162b2 induces significantly higher levels of SARS-CoV-2 specific binding and neutralizing antibody responses when compared to CoronaVac. CoronaVac induce higher CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    and CD8
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cell responses to the structural protein than BNT162b2.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Comparison of the immunogenicity of BNT162b2 and CoronaVac COVID-19 Vaccines in Hong Kong

reference.article-title: Neutralizing Antibodies Correlate with Protection from SARS-CoV-2 in Humans during a Fishery Vessel Outbreak with a High Attack Rate
